title: Bridge Essay: From nasal respiration to brain dynamic.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** (Phase 2) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Breath/Soul to mechanistic neuroscience.

**From nasal respiration to brain dynamic.** — Rev Neurosci. PMID: 38579456. DOI: 10.1515/revneuro-2023-0152. While breathing is avital, involuntary physiological function, the mode of respiration, particularly nasal breathing, exerts aprofound influence on brain activity and cognitive processes. This review synthesizes existing research on the interactions between nasal respiration and the entrainment of oscillations across brain regions involved in cognition. The rhythmic activation of olfactory sensory neurons during nasal respiration is linked to oscillations in widespread brain regions, including
